content.full_text:
UNI-GENERAL
8/1200

2024
A-19

June 22, 1977

Ms. Kathleen Evans
Association for Better Citizenship
1705 Murchison Drive
Burlingame, CA 94010

Dear Kathy:

I received your letter and attachments today and want to
thank you for putting so much time and effort into the
presentation.

The information you have provided will help us demonstrate
what a difficult task it is to meet all the various dead-
lines when an organization such as yours is involved in
numerous elections throughout the state. I will keep you
informed concerning our progress in modifying these require-
ments.

Thanks again for your assistance.

Sincerely,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Program Coordinator

RSK/gt

ABC
Association for
Better Citizenship
1705 Murchieon Drive
Burlingame, California 94010
(415) 692-3127 or (415) 697-1400

June 15, 1977

Ms. Regina Kutchins
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Regina:

In response to our telephone conversation, I would like to explain
the difficulties our Association is having in adhering to the filing
deadlines as required by the California Political Reform Act.

As you know, the Association For Better Citizenship participates in
hundreds of elections each year, many falling on different dates
(city, county, legislative, special, as well as contributing to
legislator recognition fund-raising events.)

Following is a listing of those elections in which we have partici-
pated since 1975:

1975

Filing Periods

AD 15 Special          Jan. 7 - Jan. 25        Jan. 28
                       Jan. 26 - Feb. 22       Feb. 25
                       Feb. 23 - after March 4 May    8

SD 29                  Jan. 7 - Feb. 22        Feb. 25
                       Feb. 23 - March 22      March 25
                       March 23 - May 29       June 5

AD 47                  Jan. 7 - May 12         May 15
                       May 13 - June 7         June 10
                       June 8 - July 5         July 8
                       July 6 - Sept. 11       Sept. 18

March 4 County         Jan. 7 - Feb. 17        Feb. 20
                       Feb. 18-after March 4   May 8

April 1                Jan.7 - March 22        March 25
                       March 23 - May 29       June 5

- 2 -

April 4                Jan. 7 - March 20          March 23
                       March 21 - June 1          June 8

April 15               Jan. 7 - March 31          April 3
                       April 1 - June 12          June 18

May 13                 Jan. 7 - March 8           March 11
                       March 9 - May 3            May 6
                       May 4 - July 10            July 17

May 27                 Jan. 7 - April 19          April 22
                       April 20 - May 17          May 20
                       May 18 - Aug. 2            Aug. 5

June 10                April 28 - May 26          May 29
                       May 27 - Aug. 7            Aug. 14

August 5               July 22 - Oct. 2           Oct. 9

Sept. 16               Aug. 12 - Sept. 6          Sept. 9
                       Sept. 7 - Oct. 25          Oct. 28
                       Oct. 26 - Jan. 1           Jan. 8

Dec. 2                 Jan. 7 - Oct. 25           Oct. 28
                       Oct. 26 - Nov. 22          Nov. 25
                       Nov. 23 - after Elec.      Dec. 2

Our filing procedure during 1975 was not in strict compliance with
the Act. We filed according to all the dates above, but only itemized
those contributions in connection with the respective elections. We
attached a detailed summary of receipts and expenditures for each
period with each form. Sample "A" attached. (Note: when only one
or two periods are mentioned it is because our contributions for such
elections occurred after the first or second filing period.) Our ration-
ale was that under the "Waxman-Dymally" law we had been allowed to
file in this manner after explaining our situation to the Secretary of
State's office and we continued to do so in 1975 and through June,
1976 primary.

1976

Jan. 27                Dec. 16 - Jan. 12          Jan. 15
                       Jan. 13 - Jan. 28          April 1

March 2                Jan. 1 - Jan. 19           Jan. 22
                       Jan. 20 - Feb. 16          Feb. 19
                       Feb. 17 - April 29         May 6

Feb. 24                Jan. 20 - Feb. 9           Feb. 12
                       Feb. 10 - after Feb. 24    April 29

- 3 -

May 11 Special          Jan. 1 - March 29              April 1
                        March 30 - April 26            April 29
                        April 27 - July 8              July 15

June Primary           Jan. 1 - April 26               April 29
                       April 27 - May 24               May 27
                       May 25 - Aug. 5                 Aug. 12

Nov. General           Aug. 6 - Sept. 20               Sept. 23
                       Sept. 21 - Oct. 18             Oct. 21
                       Oct. 19 - any time after       Jan. 6
                                  election*

* We closed the November reports on December 16, as all receipts
and expenditures in connection with the November election were
completed.

                              1977

ABC contributed to literally hundreds of candidates in March 8, 1977,
elections. Our filing periods were:

                       Dec. 17* - Jan. 24             Jan. 27
                       Jan. 25 - Feb. 21              Feb. 24
                       Feb. 22 - May 5                May 12

* day after the closing date of our November, 1976, election
report.

We were filing COMPLETE reports in connection with this election.
We could not close early because we anticipated additional expendi-
tures in connection with the election until May. In the meantime,
during this period we also contributed to candidates in the April 5,
April 12, April 19, May 17 and May 31 elections.

All contributions to these candidates were reported on our March 8
reports, rather than reported separately according to the deadlines
for these elections because they occured during the March 8 period.
After we closed the March 8 report, we contributed to additional
candidates for May 31 elections. We are filing complete reports
again in connection with this election, dates:

                       May 6* - May 16               May 19
                       May 17 - July 28              Aug. 4

* day after closing date for March 8 reports.

We now anticipate contributing to a June 21 Special Election candidate.
The first two filing periods are over. The closing report is through
any time after June 21--up to August 19 and is due August 26. There-
fore, we will include this expenditure on the May 31 filing period,
close July 28 and be in compliance. (???)

- 4 -

Although not required by law, we file copies of all our reports
with the Secretary of State, all county clerks in California as
well as any city clerk in a city in which we are participating
in an election. We can because if we were to adhere to the letter
of the law and only filed in three jurisdictions -- county of domicile,
county with largest population and Secretary of State (when necessary)
the filing officers in those counties not in receipt of our reports
would be unaware of the pertinate as to why they had not received
them. We would be plagued with phone calls and letters and notifi-
cations of failure to file, etc., etc..

We have been fined $450 by the City of Los Angeles for not abiding
by the April 5 filing dates. We were required to file abbreviated
statements dated 2/22 - 3/26 and 3/27 - 6/2 (closing). (See attached).

On June 7 we received a notification from the County of Los Angeles
that we are required to file an amendment to the County for an April 5
election, such amendment to be dated 5/6 - 5/21. I called Richard
Bryant, (Elections Deputy) asked why, explained our dilemma, explained
that we had reported, and he said, "forget the amendment." The
reason the County April 5 filing deadlines differed from the City
April 5 filing deadline was because it was a run-off election held
"not less than 60 days following a primary" in which case reports
are due 33 and 7 days prior etc., etc..

The purpose of the Act is to provide campaign disclosure. The filing
deadlines were written for those candidates and committees concerned
with one specific election. To require an organization such as ours
to abide by these filing requirements is causing nothing but confusion
for the filing officers in receipt of the reports and the public
inspecting the reports. Needless to say, it has caused confusion
for those of us filing the reports.

Our recommendation for organizations such as ABC is:

Monthly reports
Period covered: One month
Due: Third day of the following month

The monthly reports itemizing all receipts and expenditures would
show, on a regular basis, a true picture of expenditures and receipts
for organizations involved in numerous elections and would be in
compliance with the main purpose of the law--DISCLOSURE.

Sincerely,

Kathleen Evans
Association for Better Citizenship

KE:es

sections.question_synthetic: The Association for Better Citizenship requests guidance on filing deadline compliance under the California Political Reform Act, arguing that the current deadlines designed for single-election committees create administrative burden and confusion for multi-election organizations, and proposes adoption of monthly reporting instead.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (June 15, 1977) and FPPC acknowledgment response (June 22, 1977). The primary document is the incoming request; the FPPC response is merely an acknowledgment letter with no substantive legal advice. No formal Question or Conclusion sections present. Document contains extensive supporting documentation with election filing period tables.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from Kathleen Evans of the Association for Better Citizenship to FPPC Program Coordinator Regina Siciliano-Kutchins explaining the difficulties their organization faces in adhering to multiple filing deadlines required by the California Political Reform Act. The letter provides detailed tables of filing periods for elections in 1975-1977 and proposes a monthly reporting alternative.
